sutta: monks in the evening the lion king of beasts leaves his lair he stretches himself he looks around on the 4 quarters 3 times he roars his line raw then he goes forth to hunt monks if he strike a blow at an elephant he strikes verily with care not without care if he strikes a blow at a buffalo at an ox at the leopard if he strikes a blow at any small creature be it but a hare or cat he strikes with care not without care and why he thinks let not my skill fail me [Music] a lion monks that is a name for the arahan samasambuddha verily monks when the tata gather teaches dhamma in assembly that is his lion brawl and if he teach dhamma to the monks he teaches with care not without care if he teach dhamma to the nuns to lay men to lay women disciples if the tata gata teach dhamma to the manifold be they but fowlers who go about with grain he teaches with care not without care and why filled with respect for dhamma is the tattagata amongst filled with reverence for dhamma that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the buddha is saying just as a lion when he goes out to hunter he takes a lot of care makes sure every time he makes a kill so in the same way the buddha said when the buddha teaches the dhamma he also like the lion takes a lot of care makes sure he his message strikes home so that is that's about teaching dhamma in 1 of the sutas later we'll find the buddha says that even the buddha has a raja and the raja of the buddha or the dhamma king is the dhamma and in another sutra the buddha immediately after he was enlightened he contemplated whether there is another being higher than him in wisdom etc that he should respect that and when he looked around he saw that there's no higher being than himself then he wondered to himself whom should he respect or what should he respect then he reflected he thought dhamma is is is that which he should venerate and respect so because of veneration of the dharma whenever the buddha or his monks teach the dharma they should do so with great care not carelessly so it is very good for a monk every time he prepares a talk and i mean every time he gives a talk or something to prepare and because of that also of respect for the dhamma the buddha has made it part of the vinaya rules that when among teach dhamma to lay people they must be worthy of being taught the dhamma they have to show the respect that's why when lay people listen to the dhamma they should not sit higher than among uh this license is lower or on the same level but not higher and also they should not for example uh wear a hat and they should also not hold the knees together sit properly and uh they should not carry an umbrella or carry a stick or carry a weapon and these are all part of the denial rules that we should uh out of respect for the dhamma and be quite respectful when we hear the dhamma because there's nothing help more helpful to us than the dhamma we will realize this as we grow older and there is no no no nothing of such great value in the world as the dhamma the nexus is
